_comment: Curated risk tiers for Microsoft Graph Application (app-only) permissions, keyed by their well-known, tenant-independent appRoleId GUID. GUIDs sourced from Microsoft's official permissions-reference documentation (microsoftgraph/microsoft-graph-docs-contrib). This is a curated subset focused on permissions with documented privilege-escalation potential (see e.g. SpecterOps/Andy Robbins' Entra ID privilege escalation research) - NOT an exhaustive list of all Graph permissions. An appRoleAssignment whose GUID isn't listed here is treated as tier 0 (unclassified / not flagged), not guessed.

tiers_legend.4: Critical - can lead to full directory/tenant compromise (grant arbitrary directory roles, add credentials to any app, rewrite directory objects)
tiers_legend.3: High - broad write access enabling significant escalation or damage
tiers_legend.1: Low/moderate - broad read-only visibility, not directly escalation-enabling
